title: Removing Case-Sensitive Consecutive Duplicates from a String

statement.markdown: Given a string containing lowercase letters, write a function that removes all consecutive duplicate characters. For example, if the input string is 'aabbbcccc', the output should be 'abc'.
statement.inputFormat: A single line string input containing only lowercase letters.
statement.outputFormat: A single line string output containing no consecutive duplicate characters.
statement.notes: This function should not use any additional data structures like sets or linked lists.

hints.text: Use two pointers to track consecutive characters.

editorial.title: Optimal Solution
editorial.intuition: Iterate over the input string using two pointers.
editorial.bruteForce: Use recursion to solve the problem, but it is inefficient.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use two pointers to track consecutive characters.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function works by checking if the current character is the same as the previous one.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: string solve(string S); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public String solve(String S); };
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, S: str) -> str: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: string solve(string S) { string ans = ''; for (int i = 0; i < S.length(); i++) { if (i == 0 || S[i] != S[i-1]) ans += S[i]; } return ans; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public String solve(String S) { String ans = ''; for (int i = 0; i < S.length(); i++) { if (i == 0 || S.charAt(i) != S.charAt(i-1)) ans += S.charAt(i); } return ans; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, S: str) -> str: ans = '' for i in range(len(S)): if i == 0 or S[i] != S[i-1]: ans += S[i] return ans
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(S) { var ans = ''; for (var i = 0; i < S.length; i++) { if (i == 0 || S[i] != S[i-1]) ans += S[i]; } return ans; }

solutions.python:
class Solution: def solve(self, S: str) -> str: ans = '' for i in range(len(S)): if i == 0 or S[i] != S[i-1]: ans += S[i] return ans
import sys
S = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
print(Solution().solve(S))
